perturbation.original_text: You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy the Company may have against your Representatives with respect to such breach).
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy the Company may have against your Representatives with respect to such breach)', the legal text now introduces an ambiguity. It is no longer clear whether the Company can directly pursue the Representatives for a breach or if their only recourse is through Ultragenyx. This omission now can be interpreted that pursuing Ultragenyx for the breaches of its Representatives is the sole recourse to the Company. The affected part is Section 1, because it is the section about Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material .
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . You hereby agree that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering,negotiating and/or implementing the Possible Transaction; provided , however , that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed (i) to any of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering, negotiating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, (ii) pursuant to an External Demand in accordance with paragraph 4 of this letter agreement and (iii) as the Company may otherwise consent in writing. All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement, and (C) be advised of the terms of this letter agreement and directed to comply with the terms hereof to the same extent as if they were parties to this letter agreement. You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text clarifies that Ultragenyx's responsibility for breaches by its Representatives does not limit the Company's right to pursue the Representatives directly. The changed text removes this clarification, creating ambiguity. This omission contradicts the contradicted text, which implies that Representatives are directly subject to the terms of the agreement and potentially liable for breaches, because the Company can now only interpret that they can pursue Ultragenyx. Thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement, and (C) be advised of the terms of this letter agreement and directed to comply with the terms hereof to the same extent as if they were parties to this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (C), the Representatives are no longer explicitly directed to comply with the terms of the letter agreement. While they still agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with the agreement, the explicit directive to 'comply' is removed. This creates a contradiction, because the previous paragraph states that you (Ultragenyx) are responsible for any breaches by your Representatives. This responsibility is weakened if the Representatives are not explicitly directed to comply, but only to 'treat the material in accordance with' the agreement. This can be interpreted differently and weakens the enforcement on the Representatives, affecting the liability to Ultragenyx. This has a direct conflict in Section 1 where it states that Ultragenyx is responsible for the breaches of its representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . You hereby agree that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering,negotiating and/or implementing the Possible Transaction; provided , however , that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed (i) to any of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering, negotiating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, (ii) pursuant to an External Demand in accordance with paragraph 4 of this letter agreement and (iii) as the Company may otherwise consent in writing. All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement. You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit directive for Representatives to comply with the terms of the letter agreement, weakening the enforceability of the agreement on them. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that Ultragenyx is responsible for any breaches by its Representatives, as the representatives now only have to treat the material in accordance with the agreement, and not fully comply. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You hereby acknowledge that you and your Representatives are aware, and will advise your Representatives, that the Evaluation Material may contain material, non-public information about the Company and that United States securities laws prohibit any person who has material, non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling any securities of the Company while in possession of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: You hereby acknowledge that you and your Representatives are aware that the Evaluation Material may contain material, non-public information about the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'and will advise your Representatives, that ... United States securities laws prohibit any person who has material, non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling any securities of the Company while in possession of such information' creates an internal contradiction. While Ultragenyx acknowledges that *they* are aware of the potential for insider trading issues, there is no longer a requirement that they advise their representatives of the same. This creates uncertainty about whether the representatives are actually informed, and increases the risk of an inadvertent breach of securities laws. This affects Section 1, where it describes the non-disclosure agreement that should be maintained with the representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . You hereby agree that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering,negotiating and/or implementing the Possible Transaction; provided , however , that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed (i) to any of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering, negotiating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, (ii) pursuant to an External Demand in accordance with paragraph 4 of this letter agreement and (iii) as the Company may otherwise consent in writing. All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement. You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an explicit requirement to advise representatives about insider trading restrictions related to non-public information. The changed text omits this requirement, meaning that Ultragenyx is no longer explicitly obligated to inform its representatives about these restrictions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 1), which states that 'All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material...'. The omission creates a direct conflict, as the representatives may not be informed as required by Section 1, making the contract inconsistent and creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you further agree that you will not share the Evaluation Material with or enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding, or any discussions which would reasonably be expected to lead to an agreement, arrangement or understanding, with any other person, including other potential bidders and equity or debt financing sources (other than your Representatives as permitted above) regarding a Possible Transaction involving the Company without the prior written consent of the Company and only upon such person executing a confidentiality agreement in favor of the Company with terms and conditions substantially consistent with this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you further agree that you will not share the Evaluation Material with or enter into any agreement, arrangement or understanding, or any discussions which would reasonably be expected to lead to an agreement, arrangement or understanding, with any other person, including other potential bidders and equity or debt financing sources (other than your Representatives as permitted above) regarding a Possible Transaction involving the Company without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and only upon such person executing a confidentiality agreement in favor of the Company with terms and conditions substantially consistent with this letter agreement', the contract no longer explicitly requires a confidentiality agreement for third parties who receive Evaluation Material. This creates an in-text contradiction because earlier paragraphs establish the importance of confidentiality. Removing this clause weakens the protection of the Evaluation Material and contradicts the intent of maintaining confidentiality as outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . You hereby agree that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and used solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering,negotiating and/or implementing the Possible Transaction; provided , however , that the Evaluation Material may be disclosed (i) to any of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating, considering, negotiating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, (ii) pursuant to an External Demand in accordance with paragraph 4 of this letter agreement and (iii) as the Company may otherwise consent in writing. All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement. You agree to be responsible for any breaches of any of the provisions of this letter agreement by any of your Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text required any third party receiving Evaluation Material to execute a confidentiality agreement. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text, "Non-Disclosure and Use of Evaluation Material . You hereby agree that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential", explicitly states that the Evaluation Material must be kept confidential, which is weakened by removing the requirement for a confidentiality agreement for third parties. This creates a direct conflict by potentially allowing Evaluation Material to be shared without the same level of protection, violating the intent of confidentiality described in the contradicted section. The change makes the contract inconsistent, meeting the conditions for an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as removing essential language that contradicts constraints stated in other sections.
